name: BioRestorative Therapies, Inc.
business: BioRestorative Therapies develops therapeutic products using adult stem cells, with a lead candidate BRTX-100 in Phase 2 for chronic lower back pain from degenerative disc disease. The company also operates a commercial biocosmeceuticals business selling a cell-based secretome to reduce fine lines and wrinkles. It markets to physicians, health care professionals, hospitals, and directly to consumers via distributors and medical spas.

exposures.sentence: We are currently pursuing our Disc/Spine Program with our initial investigational therapeutic product being called BRTX-100.

We are also developing our ThermoStem Program. This pre-clinical program involves the use of brown adipose (fat) in connection with the cell-based treatment of type 2 diabetes and obesity as well as hypertension, other metabolic disorders and cardiac deficiencies.
We have also licensed an investigational curved needle device designed to deliver cells and/or other therapeutic products or material to the spine and discs (and other parts of the body).

We operate a commercial biocosmeceuticals business. Our current commercial product, formulated and manufactured using our cGMP ISO-7 certified clean room, is a cell-based secretome containing exosomes, proteins and growth factors.

catalysts.event: FDA granted Fast Track designation for BRTX-100 for chronic lumbar disc disease
catalysts.sentence: In February 2025, we announced that the FDA granted Fast Track designation to the BRTX-100 program for the treatment of chronic lumbar disc disease, or cLDD.

catalysts.event: Preliminary 26- and 52-week blinded data from first 15 patients presented
catalysts.sentence: In February 2025, we announced new preliminary 26- and 52- week blinded data from the first 15 patients with cLDD enrolled in our ongoing Phase 2 clinical trial of BRTX-100.

catalysts.event: FDA cleared IND application for BRTX-100 for chronic cervical discogenic pain
catalysts.sentence: In February 2025, we announced that the FDA cleared our IND application for BRTX-100 for the treatment of chronic cervical discogenic pain.

catalysts.event: Preliminary data from 36 patients presented at ISSCR 2025
catalysts.sentence: In June 2025, we announced that new preliminary blinded clinical data from 36 patients enrolled in our ongoing Phase 2 trial of BRTX-100 for the treatment of cLDD was presented by our Vice President of Research and Development at the International Society for Stem Cell Research 2025 Annual Meeting.

catalysts.event: Type B meeting with FDA granted to discuss accelerated BLA approval pathway
catalysts.sentence: In November 2025, we announced that we were granted a Type B meeting with the FDA to discuss a potential accelerated biologics license application, or BLA, approval pathway for the Fast-Track-Designated BRTX-100 program for the treatment of cLDD.

catalysts.event: Completed enrollment of 99 patients in Phase 2 clinical trial of BRTX-100
catalysts.sentence: In February 2026, we announced that we had completed the enrollment of 99 patients in our Phase 2 clinical trial of BRTX-100.

catalysts.event: Initiated Phase 3 enabling activities with goal to submit Phase 3 IND in 2026
catalysts.sentence: Based upon the positive official summary of the meeting, we have initiated Phase 3 enabling activities with the goal of submitting a Phase 3 IND during 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Nearly 25 million American adults have chronic lower back pain, approximately 12 million diagnosed with disc degeneration, and approximately 5.6 million have pain caused by a protruding or injured disc.
tamClaims.sentence: According to a 2016 market report from Trinity Partners, a global life sciences consulting firm, of the 250 million American adults, nearly 25 million have chronic lower back pain of which approximately 12 million have been diagnosed with and treated for disc degeneration and approximately 5.6 million have pain caused by a protruding or injured disc.

tamClaims.claim: Between 500,000 and one million invasive surgical procedures are performed each year for lower back conditions, costing approximately $40 billion.
tamClaims.sentence: We believe that between 500,000 and one million invasive surgical procedures are performed each year to try to alleviate the pain associated with these lower back conditions and that such procedures cost approximately $40 billion.
